mnemonics: CAP Theorem: Choose any two - Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance. Remember 'CAP' as 'Can't All Please' to recall the trade-off.
keyPoints: Consensus algorithms like Paxos and Raft are fundamental for achieving agreement in distributed systems.
Distributed transactions often use the Two-Phase Commit (2PC) protocol to ensure atomicity across nodes.
Understanding the trade-offs of the CAP Theorem (Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance) is crucial for system design.
commonMistakes: Assuming strong consistency is possible or required in all distributed system scenarios, ignoring the CAP theorem.
Confusing horizontal scaling (adding more nodes) with vertical scaling (adding more power to a single node).
Overlooking the importance of idempotency in designing retry mechanisms for network calls.
